codeReview.summary: The fix appropriately marks 4 tests in tests/unit/plugin-tools-hook.test.ts as skipped with detailed comments explaining they require authenticated cursor-agent. The root cause is CursorPlugin() initialization which triggers autoRefreshModels() that runs `cursor-agent models` with a 5s timeout. Without authenticated cursor-agent, these hang. The remaining 3 tests pass and cover core plugin tool hook functionality.

fulfillsAssertions: bun test runs without test failures (493 pass, 5 skip, 0 fail)
Plugin tests are skipped with clear reason (detailed skip comments explain cursor-agent dependency)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skip comments are more detailed than typical skip reasons. They explain both the symptom (hanging on cursor-agent models) and the root cause (ensureCursorProxyServer behavior when proxy has no workspaceDirectory). This level of detail is appropriate for integration-style tests that require external auth.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/unit/plugin-tools-hook.test.ts:28-33 - The first skip comment spans 6 lines explaining the full root cause

sharedStateObservations.observation: No blocking issues found. The approach of using it.skip() with clear comments is a standard pattern for integration tests that require external dependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The tests follow bun test's native skip mechanism

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered a subtle bug: when a proxy is already running on port 32124 without workspaceDirectory in health response, ensureCursorProxyServer tries to start a new proxy which hangs on cursor-agent. This explains why tests might pass/fail inconsistently depending on proxy state. This knowledge is already captured in the skip comment.

summary: The fix-plugin-tests feature correctly addresses the pre-existing test failures by marking 4 tests that require authenticated cursor-agent as skipped with clear, detailed reason comments. All tests pass (3 pass, 4 skip, 0 fail). Typecheck and lint pass. The skip reasons are appropriate and explain both the symptom and root cause.
